title: Teaching a University Class on Evil
subtitle: 当大学开了一门关于「邪恶」的课

paragraphs.en: A few years ago I found myself teaching a university class on evil. It was for third-year criminology students to help them contextualize theory and research within controversial current topics. It was a huge success. The debates were heated and interesting. I could see people's views change within the course of a single lecture.
paragraphs.zh: 几年前，我在大学开设了一门关于「恶」的课程，面向三年级犯罪学学生，帮助他们将理论和研究置于当下有争议的话题中。课程取得了巨大成功，讨论热烈而精彩。我能看到人们的观点在一堂课的时间里就发生了转变。

words.meaning: v. 将……置于背景中理解

paragraphs.en: Over the past 13 years, as a student, lecturer and researcher, I've enjoyed discussing the science of evil with anyone willing to listen. What I like most is destroying the cliché of good and evil, and replacing them with scientific insight. We need a more informed way of discussing behavior that at first we cannot, or should not, begin to understand.
paragraphs.zh: 过去13年里，作为学生、讲师和研究者，我乐于与任何愿意倾听的人探讨「恶」的科学。我最喜欢做的事就是打破善与恶的刻板印象，代之以科学的洞见。我们需要一种更理性的方式来讨论那些乍看之下我们无法、也不应该轻易理解的行为。

words.meaning: n. 陈词滥调；刻板印象

paragraphs.en: Without understanding, we risk dehumanizing others, writing off human beings simply because we don't comprehend them. We must try to understand what we have labeled evil. We tend to think evil is something that other people are.
paragraphs.zh: 如果不理解，我们就有可能将他人非人化，仅仅因为不理解他们就将其一笔勾销。我们必须尝试去理解那些被我们贴上邪恶标签的东西。我们往往觉得邪恶是别人身上的特质。

words.meaning: v. 使非人化；使丧失人性

paragraphs.en: We think of ourselves as "good people", and even when we do morally wrong things, we understand the context of our decisions. With others, however, it is far easier to write them off. If their actions deviate substantially from what we consider acceptable, we may label them evil.
paragraphs.zh: 我们认为自己是「好人」，即使我们做了道德上错误的事情，我们也理解我们做出决定的背景。然而，对于其他人，我们却很容易将他们一笔勾销。如果他们的行为严重偏离我们认为可接受的范围，我们可能会给他们贴上邪恶的标签。

paragraphs.en: We need to be careful with this. Calling someone evil is often similar to saying they cannot change, and perhaps aren't even a human at all. However, when you actually go monster-hunting, and you look deeply at the people behind shocking behavior, you may be surprised.
paragraphs.zh: 我们需要小心这一点。称某人为邪恶通常类似于说他们无法改变，甚至可能根本不是人。然而，当你真正去追寻所谓的怪物，深入审视那些骇人行为背后的人时，你可能会大吃一惊。

paragraphs.en: As a child I used to love the Scooby-Doo cartoons. Arriving in their "Mystery Machine", the gang would have to find a monster who was terrorizing a neighborhood. They would run around looking for clues and at the end unmask the bad guy. It was always a normal person in a costume. There were no monsters.
paragraphs.zh: 小时候我很喜欢史酷比动画片。一伙人开着「神秘机器」到达现场，必须找到正在侵扰社区的怪物。他们四处寻找线索，最终揭开坏人的面具。结果每次都是一个穿着戏服的普通人，根本没有怪物。

paragraphs.en: Like the Scooby crew, we may find ourselves hunting for an easy fix, one word for people who do bad things. But if we take a good look, the word 'evil' is insufficient — there are no simple explanations for why humans do bad things: instead there are many, and they are all marvelously different.
paragraphs.zh: 和史酷比小队一样，我们也可能在寻找一个简单的标签来概括做坏事的人。但如果我们认真审视，会发现「邪恶」这个词远远不够——人类为什么做坏事没有简单的解释，原因有很多，而且各不相同。

paragraphs.en: Evil is typically referred to when there is deviance from social norms: formal deviance is the violation of laws, like theft, murder, and attacks, while informal deviance involves violations of social norms, like lying. Evil behavior is typically thought to embrace one or both forms. However, deviance can also describe a behavior that simply differs from the norm.
paragraphs.zh: 邪恶通常指的是偏离社会规范的行为：正式的越轨是违反法律，如盗窃、谋杀和攻击；而非正式的越轨则涉及违反社会规范，如撒谎。邪恶行为通常被认为包含其中一种或两种形式。然而，越轨也可以用来描述一种仅仅是与常规不同的行为。

paragraphs.en: Perhaps this is where we can find the good side of our bad side. Deviating from the norm can make us villains, but it can also make us heroes. A child deviates from social pressures when they stand up for another child being bullied in school. A soldier deviates when they choose not to follow orders to kill an innocent civilian. An employee in a big tech company deviates when they expose its wrongdoings.
paragraphs.zh: 也许这正是我们能从自身的「坏」中发现「好」的地方。偏离常规可以让我们成为恶棍，但也可以让我们成为英雄。一个孩子为在学校被欺负的同学挺身而出，这是对社会压力的偏离。一名士兵拒绝执行杀害无辜平民的命令，这也是偏离。一家大型科技公司的员工揭露公司的不当行为，同样是偏离。

paragraphs.en: Creativity is also a deviation. Here, too, things are complex. Thinking creatively has given us modern medicine, technology and modern political structures, but it has also given us poison and nuclear weapons. Great benefit and great harm can come from the same human tendency.
paragraphs.zh: 创造力也是一种偏离。这里的情况同样复杂。创造性思维给了我们现代医学、科技和现代政治制度，但也给了我们毒药和核武器。巨大的益处和巨大的伤害可能源自人类同一种天性。

paragraphs.en: In a research paper, Evil Genius, published in 2014, the behavioral scientists Francesca Gino and Scott Wiltermuth wanted to examine whether people who behave unethically in one task are more creative than others on a subsequent task, even after controlling for differences in baseline creative skills. The unethical behavior they chose was dishonesty.
paragraphs.zh: 在一篇发表于2014年的研究论文《邪恶天才》中，行为科学家弗兰切斯卡·吉诺和斯科特·威尔特穆思希望研究：在一项任务中行为不道德的人，在后续任务中是否会比其他人更有创造力——即使控制了创造力基线水平的差异。他们选择的不道德行为是不诚实。

paragraphs.en: Over five experiments researchers gave participants tasks in which they could cheat. In one study, they were given matrixes and had to find two numbers that added up to 10. Participants were asked to self-report how well they did at the end of the study: 59% cheated by saying that they solved more matrixes than they actually had.
paragraphs.zh: 在五项实验中，研究人员给参与者布置了可以作弊的任务。其中一项实验中，参与者拿到一组矩阵，需要找出两个相加等于10的数字。实验结束时要求参与者自行报告成绩：59%的人虚报了自己解出的矩阵数量。

paragraphs.en: After each task, the researchers measured participants' performance on the Remote Associates Test. This shows participants three words at a time that appear to be unrelated, and the person has to think of a fourth word that is associated with all of them. For example, you might get "Fox, Man, Peep", or "Dust, Cereal, Fish". In order to find the linking words ("Hole" for the first, "Bowl" for the second) you need to be creative. The more you get right, the more creative you are thought to be because you have come up with uncommon associations.
paragraphs.zh: 每项任务完成后，研究人员用远程联想测试来衡量参与者的创造力。这个测试每次向参与者展示三个看似无关的词，参与者需要想出一个与这三个词都相关的第四个词。例如，给你Fox、Man、Peep，或者Dust、Cereal、Fish。要找到连接词（第一组是Hole，第二组是Bowl），你必须发挥创造力。答对得越多，说明你越有创造力，因为你能想出不寻常的联想。

paragraphs.en: For every one of the five studies, they found the same thing — participants who cheated in the first task did better on the creativity task. Why? Like other forms of unethical behavior, lying means breaking rules. It involves being deviant, going against the social principle that people should tell the truth. Similarly, being creative involves "thinking outside the box", deviating from expectations. They involve similar thought patterns, so stimulating one stimulates the other.
paragraphs.zh: 对于五项研究中的每一项，他们都发现了同样的事情——在第一项任务中作弊的参与者在创造力任务中表现更好。为什么？与其他形式的不道德行为一样，撒谎意味着违反规则。它涉及偏差，违背人们应该说实话的社会原则。同样，创造性意味着「跳出框框思考」，偏离预期。它们涉及相似的思维模式，因此刺激一种会刺激另一种。

paragraphs.en: Can we learn from this? Perhaps. To be more creative, we could try lying in a controlled environment. Find online logic games and cheat at them, play Scrabble with a dictionary, or write a story about something that is untrue. Such tasks can get our brains thinking flexibly, beyond our normal comfort zone. This is not a call to become a compulsive liar, but a controlled liar.
paragraphs.zh: 我们可以从中学习吗？也许。为了更有创意，我们可以尝试在受控的环境中说谎。寻找在线逻辑游戏并作弊，用字典玩拼字游戏，或者写一个关于不真实事物的故事。这些任务可以让我们的大脑灵活思考，超越我们正常的舒适区。这并不是要求成为强迫性的说谎者，而是成为受控的说谎者。

words.meaning: adj. 强迫性的；难以控制的

paragraphs.en: In addition to benefits for creativity, deviance can be a good thing in other ways. Even Philip Zimbardo, the author of the Stanford prison experiment, who showed how easily we can be led to behave badly, believes that the future of deviance research may lie more in understanding extreme pro-social behavior, such as heroism. Like evil, we often view heroism as only a possibility for outliers — for people who are abnormal. But Zimbardo asks: "What if the capability to act heroically is also fundamentally ordinary and available to all of us?"
paragraphs.zh: 除了对创造力的益处，偏离在其他方面也可能是好事。斯坦福监狱实验的设计者菲利普·津巴多曾展示我们多么容易被引导去做坏事，但他也认为，越轨研究的未来可能更多地在于理解极端的亲社会行为，比如英雄主义。与邪恶一样，我们常认为英雄主义只属于异类——那些不正常的人。但津巴多问道：「如果英雄行为的能力本质上也是普通的，每个人都具备呢？」

paragraphs.en: Some say we should never meet our heroes, lest they disappoint us when we find out how normal they are. But this should be liberating, not disappointing. We are all capable of behaving like outliers. It's time for us to understand deviance, and realize its potential for good as well as for harm.
paragraphs.zh: 有人说我们不该见到自己的英雄，免得发现他们多么普通而失望。但这应该是解放，而不是失望。我们每个人都有能力成为异类。是时候让我们真正理解偏离，并认识到它既有行善的潜力，也有作恶的可能。
